content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

All three amendments target real, verified defects in the planned-elsewhere validation pipeline. Code inspection confirms: (1) extract_pe_bead_ids has zero test coverage despite gating PE validation, (2) restart_required is computed from raw mapped_to_bead_id presence at final_review.rs:1569 before any validation runs, and the validator at engine.rs:7633 explicitly skips validation when allowed_pe_ids is empty — which is the normal case for the prose-only 'no PE items' prompt, (3) reconstruct_missing_pe_mappings at success_reconciliation.rs:903-1036 reads from committed aggregates and never calls extract_pe_bead_ids or validates against the allowed set, creating a second bypass path. Together these allow a hallucinated-but-real bead ID to suppress restarts and persist through reconciliation.

## Votes

- `fr-17-fc75ff73`: Accept
  Code inspection confirms extract_pe_bead_ids (task_prompt_contract.rs:277-306) has zero dedicated tests. The function has non-trivial parsing logic — section header matching, bullet prefix stripping, splitting on whitespace/parens to extract IDs, and stopping at the next ## heading. It is the sole source of the allowed PE set that gates validation in record_planned_elsewhere_amendments. A parsing bug here (e.g., failing to match the section header due to whitespace, or not stopping at the next heading) would silently produce an empty set, which per the current engine.rs:7633 logic causes ALL mapped_to_bead_id values to be accepted without validation. The proposed test cases directly cover the realistic edge cases for this parsing logic.
- `fr-17-59a68d4c`: Accept
  Verified in code. The restart_required flag is set at final_review.rs:1569-1573 purely by checking whether any accepted amendment has mapped_to_bead_id as None — no validation of the bead ID value occurs at this point. The post-commit validator at engine.rs:7633 explicitly skips validation when allowed_pe_ids is empty ('if !allowed_pe_ids.is_empty() && ...'), and the normal no-PE-items prompt text (service.rs:1047-1050) is prose without bullets, causing extract_pe_bead_ids to return an empty set. This is not a theoretical edge case — it's the default path when no PE items are configured. Fail-closed semantics (empty allowed set means no PE classification accepted) is the correct fix, and validation must occur before restart_required is committed.
- `fr-17-1292e667`: Accept
  Verified that reconstruct_missing_pe_mappings (success_reconciliation.rs:903-1036) reads directly from committed aggregate payloads and reconstructs PlannedElsewhereMapping entries using only deduplication checks (seen_keys) and non-empty mapped_to_bead_id checks. It never calls extract_pe_bead_ids or validates against the allowed PE set. This creates a distinct bypass: even if record_planned_elsewhere_amendments correctly rejected an invalid mapping, the invalid classification persists in the committed aggregate (since restart_required was already decided), and reconstruct_missing_pe_mappings faithfully recreates it into milestone state. The fix must either sanitize the aggregate before commit (stripping invalid PE classifications) or add validation to the reconstruction path — ideally both for defense in depth.
